DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Corban Porter, Esq. of Wood Smith Henning & Berman LLP, Tel. 925.222.3419, for defendant and moving party Curtis Crane, M.D.;
Nancy Hersh, Esq. of Hersh & Hersh, Tel. 415.444.5544, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CURTIS CRANE's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT as follows: Defendant Crane's demurrers to the first and second causes of action are overruled. Even if the court accepts Dr. Crane's premise that failure to obtain informed consent is a species of medical negligence, plaintiff's theories of recovery (negligent surgery and failure to obtain informed consent) are not duplicative. There is no practical purpose in requiring plaintiff to replead his failure to obtain informed consent claim under the rubric of medical negligence in the first cause of action. Dr. Crane is not prejudiced by any purported pleading defect. The demurrers to the intentional and negligent misrepresentation claims are overruled. Stone v. Foster (1980) 106 Cal.App.3d 334, 347 does not entirely foreclose a fraud claim based on pre-operative statements and plaintiff's allegation that Dr. Crane falsely stated that he never previously had any complications support the claims. The claims are alleged with sufficient particularity and less facts are necessary where the defendant has presumptive knowledge of the claim. Plaintiff correctly notes that he is allowed to plead inconsistent theories of recovery. (See Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR # 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
